record.field: MariaDB is a fast, MySQL-compatible open-source relational database for CMSs, CRMs and business apps that expect a MySQL backend.

MariaDB is a fast, MySQL-compatible open-source relational database for CMSs, CRMs and business apps that expect a MySQL backend.

Created by the original authors of MySQL, MariaDB is a community-developed, GPL-licensed relational database that stays wire- and SQL-compatible with MySQL, so most MySQL applications connect to it unchanged. It is a proven backing store for CMSs, CRMs and administrative systems, and is often the better-tested option for apps that do not support PostgreSQL. This standalone deployment runs on the official image and provisions an application database and a dedicated user on first boot, ready for other services to connect over the internal network.

Key features
  • MySQL wire and SQL compatibility, so most MySQL apps run without changes
  • Pluggable storage engines including InnoDB for reliable ACID transactions
  • Query optimizer, thread pool and parallel replication for strong performance
  • Official Docker image with unattended setup through MARIADB_* environment variables
  • Automatic creation of an application database and a dedicated user on first start
  • Community-driven, GPL-licensed and a drop-in successor to MySQL
Best for

Teams that need a proven MySQL-compatible database to back apps such as Matomo, SuiteCRM, EspoCRM, WordPress or LimeSurvey, or any service that is better tested against MySQL/MariaDB than PostgreSQL. Deploy it once and connect apps over the internal network with per-app credentials, never exposing port 3306 to the Internet.

services: mariadb: image: mariadb:11 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped environment: MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD: {{ root_password }} MARIADB_DATABASE: {{ db_name }} MARIADB_USER: {{ db_user }} MARIADB_PASSWORD: {{ db_password }} volumes: - ./data:/var/lib/mysql expose: - 3306 networks: - internal healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "healthcheck.sh", "--connect", "--innodb_initialized"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 5 networks: internal: driver: bridge

#!/bin/bash # Dump lógico de la base de aplicación. Corre dentro del contenedor con las credenciales # del entorno (MARIADB_*), nunca inline. MariaDB 11 usa el binario `mariadb-dump`. set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c \ 'exec mariadb-dump -uroot -p"$MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD" --single-transaction --databases "$MARIADB_DATABASE"' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql" echo "mariadb-dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.sql") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash # Restaura el dump lógico. Levanta la BD, espera readiness y reimporta el .sql; luego # verifica que la base de aplicación quedó con al menos una tabla. set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d mariadb for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c 'exec mariadb -uroot -p"$MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD" -e "SELECT 1"' >/dev/null 2>&1 && break sleep 2 done cat "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.sql" | docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c 'exec mariadb -uroot -p"$MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD"' N=$(docker compose exec -T mariadb sh -c 'exec mariadb -uroot -p"$MARIADB_ROOT_PASSWORD" -N -e "SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema=\"$MARIADB_DATABASE\""') echo "restored tables: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
Password for the MariaDB root superuser.
Application database created on first boot.
Application database user created on first boot.
Password for the application database user.
